title: rubus cenomanensis
intro: Rubus cenomanensis is an extinct species of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae), specifically a type of wild raspberry. The medicinal properties of Rubus cenomanensis are not well-documented, but its modern relatives, such as the common raspberry and blackberry, are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of the plant's relatives include lowering cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and providing anti-cancer properties. Although the exact herbal preparations of Rubus cenomanensis are unknown, its modern relatives are often consumed as fresh fruits, juices, jams, and teas, or used in supplements and tinctures.
